title: A resolution condemning the commutation of the death sentence of Marvin Charles Gabrion II granted by President Biden on December 23, 2024.
stage_label: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary. (text: CR S140)
plain_summary: This resolution is the Senate's way of strongly disagreeing with President Biden's decision to change a death sentence to life in prison. The person affected is Marvin Charles Gabrion II, who was convicted of serious crimes including murder and rape. The Senate believes President Biden made this decision for political reasons rather than good reasons. They think this decision was unfair to the victims and their families who were hurt by Gabrion's crimes.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary. (text: CR S140)
subjects: Presidents and presidential powers, Vice Presidents

sections.title: Section 1  Sense of the Senate Regarding the Commutation of the Death Sentence of Marvin Charles Gabrion II Granted by President Biden on December 23, 2024
sections.text: The Senate believes that President Joseph R. Biden hurt the legal system and denied justice to victims when he changed the death sentence of Marvin Charles Gabrion II to life in prison on December twenty third, two thousand twenty four. Marvin Gabrion was sentenced to death for killing nineteen year old Rachel Timmerman just two days before she was supposed to testify in court that Gabrion had kidnapped and raped her. Marvin Gabrion was also the main suspect in the disappearance and murder of several other people, including Rachel Timmerman's eleven month old daughter and two people who might have been witnesses at his rape trial. This decision to change his sentence is a terrible insult to the victims of Marvin Gabrion. President Biden said he changed the death sentences of Marvin Gabrion and thirty six other murderers because he opposed the death penalty on principle, but he refused to change the death sentences of the three most controversial death row inmates. This shows that President Biden was motivated by politics, not principles. The Senate completely condemns this decision to change the sentence.
